findings.evidence: Canonical-split API evidence is now present (`qwen-turbo`, `qwen-plus-latest`, `gpt-4o-mini`, `gpt-4.1-mini`), but robustness evidence is still concentrated in one prompt policy.
findings.action: Add one additional robustness control (prompt-constrained or policy-constrained) and one more strong frontier-style control before oral-level framing.

findings.evidence: Bootstrap CI tables now exist for local and API headline comparisons, but uncertainty is not yet propagated to all figure-level delta narratives.
findings.action: Add CI/error bars to remaining delta figures and reference uncertainty directly in prose.

findings.evidence: The canonical split has full action coverage but slice imbalance remains (`answerable_control=106`, `conflicting_evidence=94`), which can weaken per-slice generalization claims.
findings.action: Close these slice gaps with human-validated additions before making balanced per-slice claims.

next_actions: Complete one additional robustness control on `data/processed/emnlp2026_expanded_dev_with_answer_topup.jsonl` (prompt-constrained and/or policy-constrained) and report with the same table/figure protocol.
Extend CI-aware reporting to all headline delta figures, not only table-level summaries.
Close remaining slice imbalances in `answerable_control` and `conflicting_evidence` with signed validation before expanding any new slice-level claims.
Expand references from 26 toward 35-40 with targeted citations in benchmark/protocol/results sections.
